notes: VAL-SETTINGS-004 started from the signed-out gate with the configured OAuth client ID cleared in Settings before submit.

VAL-SETTINGS-007 used the same dedicated session after clearing browser storage again, then saved a public sheet ID with blank OAuth client ID to reach the read-only dashboard surface without sign-in.

assertions.title: Invalid sheet input blocks save when OAuth bootstrap is unavailable

steps.action: Open Settings from the signed-out surface in a fresh session, clear the OAuth Client ID field on Setup, switch to Sheet, enter `not-a-sheet`, and click `Save & reload`.
steps.expected: Settings should stay open, show remediation on the Sheet tab, and avoid a silent reload/reset when neither a valid sheet ID nor a usable OAuth bootstrap path is present.
steps.observed: The app closed Settings and reloaded back to `http://localhost:8080/`, showing only `Open Settings`, `Create OAuth client`, and `Reload`. No inline sheet remediation was shown and the Sheet tab was no longer visible.

steps.action: Inspect persisted browser state after the reload.
steps.expected: The invalid submission should not silently reload away from the user or hide the remediation path.
steps.observed: `command_center_config_overrides` persisted the invalid `sheetId: "not-a-sheet"` plus blank `oauthClientId`, so the flow silently reloaded away from Settings instead of holding the user on the Sheet tab with remediation.

evidence.network: Clicking `Save & reload` triggered a full-page reload back to `http://localhost:8080/`; no sheet-validation request or in-modal remediation event was observed.

assertions.issues: Fail: invalid/blank sheet input with no usable OAuth bootstrap path still exited Settings and reloaded to the top-level access surface instead of keeping the Sheet tab open with remediation.

assertions.title: Discovery-focused settings entry points land on the discovery setup surface

steps.action: From a clean session, clear app storage, remove the OAuth client ID in Settings, save public sheet `1mGJ04E3f2Tp0-7ErNlb8veXjnlKz3x5a6gwyzEFvnKQ`, land on the read-only dashboard, and click the dashboard button `Open discovery setup wizard`.
steps.expected: The dashboard entry point should open the discovery setup surface or wizard for the current flow.
steps.observed: Nothing visible changed: the URL stayed `http://localhost:8080/`, the dashboard remained in place, and `#discoveryWizard` was still hidden.

steps.action: Open `Settings and setup`, switch to the `Discovery` tab, and click `Open discovery setup`.
steps.expected: The Settings entry point should land on a discovery-focused setup/remediation surface rather than a dead-end.
steps.observed: The modal stayed on the same Discovery tab with the same controls visible, the URL did not change, `#discoveryWizard` stayed hidden, and no run-discovery modal opened.

evidence.network: Post-click request inspection showed only the already-loaded discovery scripts/bootstrap GETs (`discovery-wizard-*.js`, `settings-discovery-adapters.js`, `discovery-local-bootstrap.json`); neither entry point triggered navigation, POSTs, or a visible discovery setup surface.

assertions.issues: Fail: both tested discovery-focused entry points behaved like no-ops instead of landing on the intended discovery setup surface.

summary: Retested 2 assertions in a dedicated no-auth browser session: VAL-SETTINGS-004 failed because invalid sheet input silently reloaded out of Settings instead of keeping Sheet remediation visible, and VAL-SETTINGS-007 failed because both the dashboard and Settings discovery entry points were no-ops.
